summary: Matched a large block of common fighter landing, squat, squat-wait, squat-reverse, and capture-cut code in `src/melee/ft/ft_0D14.c`, replacing address-style stubs with named C implementations and propagating the new names through many fighter call sites. decomp.dev reported 34 new matches in `main/melee/ft/ft_0D14`, +3688 matched bytes, and overall matched code +0.10%. The PR also refined common/fighter data types and motion-variable fields needed by the matches.
what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed `ft_0D14.c` functions for landing entry/animation/IASA/physics/collision: `ftCo_Landing_Enter`, `ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic`, `ftCo_LandingFallSpecial_Enter_Basic`, `ftCo_LandingFallSpecial_Enter`, `ftCo_Landing_Anim`, `ftCo_Landing_IASA`, `ftCo_Landing_Phys`, and `ftCo_Landing_Coll`.

Implemented common squat state helpers and callbacks: `ftCo_Squat_CheckInput`, `fn_800D5F84`, `ftCo_800D5FB0`, `ftCo_Squat_Enter`, `ftCo_Squat_Anim`, `ftCo_Squat_IASA`, `ftCo_Squat_Phys`, `ftCo_Squat_Coll`, `ftCo_SquatWait_CheckInput`, `fn_800D62C4`, `ftCo_800D638C`, `ftCo_SquatWait_Anim`, `ftCo_SquatWait_IASA`, `ftCo_SquatWait_Phys`, `ftCo_SquatWait_Coll`, `ftCo_SquatRv_CheckInput`, `ftCo_SquatRv_Enter`, `ftCo_SquatRv_Anim`, `ftCo_SquatRv_IASA`, `ftCo_SquatRv_Phys`, and `ftCo_SquatRv_Coll`.

Implemented capture-cut state entry/physics/collision helpers: `ftCo_CaptureCut_Enter`, `ftCo_CaptureCut_Phys`, `ftCo_CaptureCut_Coll`, `fn_800DC8D8`, and `fn_800DC8FC`; `ftCo_800DC920` was changed to a `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` body rather than a full match.

Renamed several GALE01 symbols from address-style names to semantic names, including `ftCo_800C5F88` to `ftCo_HammerLanding_Enter`, `ftCo_800D5AEC` to `ftCo_Landing_Enter`, `ftCo_800D5BF8` to `ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic`, `ft_800D5C54` to `ftCo_LandingFallSpecial_Enter_Basic`, `ftCo_800D5CB0` to `ftCo_LandingFallSpecial_Enter`, `fn_800D600C` to `ftCo_Squat_Enter`, and `ftCo_800DC750` to `ftCo_CaptureCut_Enter`.

Updated many fighter-specific collision/landing call sites to use the new common landing and capture-cut entry names, including Captain, Fox, Game & Watch, Link, Luigi, Mario, Marth, Mewtwo, Ness, Peach, Pikachu, Popo, Samus, Sheik, Zelda, Donkey, Master Hand, and common fighter files.

Added `mv.co.squat` and `mv.co.landing` fields in `ftCommon_MotionVars`, including `squat.x0`, `squat.x4`, and `landing.allow_interrupt`, matching how the newly implemented functions access motion variables.
Refined `ftCommonData` fields used by squat/capture-cut logic, changing `x90`, `x94`, `x36C`, and `x370` to floats, and typed `ftData.x28` as `WaitStruct*` plus `ftData_80085FD4_ret.x8` as `u32`.
Changed Kirby fighter variables `x64` and `xC4` from `UNK_T` to `bool`, consistent with landing cleanup code that resets Kirby-specific flags.

Added includes needed by the matched code, such as attack check headers, `ftCo_Dash`, `ftCo_Guard`, `ftCo_HammerLanding`, `ftCo_Pass`, `ftCo_SpecialS`, `ftCo_Turn`, `ftCo_Walk`, Link/Young Link helpers, Peach helper, `ftdata`, `ftwaitanim`, and `mpcoll`.
smart_moves: Propagated semantic names through call sites instead of only matching `ft_0D14.c`, making argument roles visible: e.g. `ftCo_Landing_Enter(gobj, ftCo_MS_Landing, false, Ft_MF_None, 0.0F, 1.0F)` is much clearer than `ftCo_800D5AEC(gobj, 42, 0, 0, 0, 1)`.

Introduced wrapper entry points (`ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic`, `ftCo_LandingFallSpecial_Enter_Basic`, `ftCo_LandingFallSpecial_Enter`) so call sites do not have to duplicate the hammer-landing special case or landing animation-speed calculation.
Matched complex IASA functions using repeated `RETURN_IF(...)` input checks in the observed priority order, which preserves early-return control flow while keeping the code readable.
Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in functions where stack shape mattered, such as `ftCo_Landing_IASA`, `ftCo_Squat_IASA`, and `ftCo_CaptureCut_Phys`.
Used small static/inline helpers for matching structure, notably `ftCo_Squat_IASA_inline` and `ftCo_SquatWait_Enter_inline`, rather than flattening every repeated sequence.

Typed common data and motion-variable fields just enough to express comparisons and arithmetic naturally (`float` thresholds/friction/velocity, `bool allow_interrupt`) and to remove magic `UNK_T` usage from matched logic.
Kept a known non-match explicit: `ftCo_800DC920` is present as `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`, so the PR does not pretend that all nearby capture-cut logic was fully decompiled.
decomp_lessons: When a central common-state function is matched, immediately update all callers to the semantic API; this can reveal argument types (`FtMotionId`, `MotionFlags`, `bool`, `float`) and reduce magic constants across the tree.

Landing-state decomp benefits from distinguishing full entry functions from convenience wrappers: generic entry takes `msid`, interrupt permission, flags, animation start, and animation speed, while basic/fall-special wrappers encode default state and hammer exceptions.

Common fighter state implementations often require character-specific cleanup inside a generic function. `ftCo_Landing_Enter` resets Mario/Dr. Mario, Peach, Marth/Roy, Game & Watch, Ice Climbers, Kirby, and Mewtwo flags in a switch.

Do not leave shared struct fields as `UNK_T` once new code proves their type. The PR shows float thresholds in `ftCommonData`, bool motion flags, and `WaitStruct*` typing enabling cleaner matched code.

IASA matching often depends on exact input-priority order. The landing/squat/squat-wait/squat-reverse implementations preserve a long sequence of attack, catch, jump, dash, turn, walk, and special checks.
Character-specific helper calls can be required inside common-state code, as shown by Link/Young Link logic in squat-wait entry and Peach logic in landing entry.
Preserve evidence of unresolved areas. A `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` body for `ftCo_800DC920` is better than inferring behavior from neighboring matches.
naming_conventions: Common fighter state entry functions use `ftCo_<State>_Enter`, e.g. `ftCo_Landing_Enter`, `ftCo_HammerLanding_Enter`, `ftCo_CaptureCut_Enter`, `ftCo_Squat_Enter`, and `ftCo_SquatRv_Enter`.
Simple wrappers that choose defaults are named with `_Enter_Basic`, e.g. `ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic` and `ftCo_LandingFallSpecial_Enter_Basic`.
Common action callbacks follow the state-machine suffixes `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
Motion-variable union members mirror common states, e.g. `mv.co.squat` and `mv.co.landing`, with semantic fields where known such as `landing.allow_interrupt`.
The PR converts address-style symbol names to semantic names when behavior is established, while leaving still-unknown helpers as `fn_...` or `ftCo_800...`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK` to preserve stack layout in specific functions.
Used `RETURN_IF` macro chains to match repeated early-return IASA control flow.
Used `false`, `true`, `0.0F`, and `1.0F` at call sites to force the intended bool/float argument types and improve matching/readability over raw `0`/`1`.
Kept the `flags` local in `ftCo_HammerLanding_Enter` with a nearby todo noting it cannot be moved below `fp`, suggesting inline/order sensitivity in that match.
Used inline helpers to reproduce duplicated code sequences without expanding them at every call site.
Preserved an irregular `switch` shape in squat-wait entry for Link/Young Link helper calls, with an inline comment `/* irregular */` in the diff.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 40.87%, with +0.10% and +3688 bytes.

decomp.dev reported 34 new matches, all in `main/melee/ft/ft_0D14`; largest listed new matches included `ftCo_Landing_IASA` (+416 bytes), `ftCo_Squat_IASA` (+388), `ftCo_SquatWait_IASA` (+272), `ftCo_Landing_Enter` (+268), and `ftCo_SquatRv_IASA` (+240).
decomp.dev also reported two small improvements in still-unmatched functions: `ftCo_800DC920` (+5 bytes, 0.43%) and `ftCo_800DE9D8` (+5 bytes, 7.00%).
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs completing `ftCo_800DC920` and `ftCo_800DE9D8`, which this PR only improved or stubbed.
Search for subsequent renames of remaining `fn_800D5F84`, `fn_800D62C4`, `ftCo_800D638C`, and other address-style helpers near the squat block.
Compare later landing-state PRs for further cleanup of character-specific fields reset in `ftCo_Landing_Enter`.
Check whether the `ftCo_HammerLanding_Enter` todo about inline/order sensitivity was resolved in a later matching pass.
Search for future struct typing updates to the remaining `ftCommonData` and fighter variable fields touched by landing, squat, and capture-cut logic.
